_comment: Source: Michigan MDHHS Section 1-7 Adrenal Crisis (Revised 05/08/2023, MDHHS Reviewed 2023). Base Michigan template — MCA-specific selections not configured.
protocol_reference: Michigan General Treatment Protocol Section 1-7: Adrenal Crisis — MDHHS Approved 5/8/23, Reviewed 2023
purpose: Intended for management of patients with a known history of adrenal insufficiency experiencing signs of crisis.
indications: Patient has a known history of adrenal insufficiency or Addison's disease
Presents with signs and symptoms of adrenal crisis: pallor, headache, weakness, dizziness, nausea and vomiting, hypotension, hypoglycemia, heart failure, decreased mental status, or abdominal pain

sections.title: Adrenal Crisis Treatment
points: Pediatric patients (< 14 years of age): utilize MI MEDIC cards for appropriate medication dosage; when unavailable, use pediatric dosing listed within protocol
CONTACT MEDICAL CONTROL for all adrenal crisis patients prior to treatment
Administer NS or LR IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill — refer to Vascular Access and IV Fluid Therapy-Procedure Protocol): Adults: up to 1 liter; Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg
Assist with administration of patient's own hydrocortisone sodium succinate (Solu-Cortef) (Specialist skill): Adults: 100 mg IV/IM; Pediatrics: 1–2 mg/kg IV/IM

If patient does not have their own hydrocortisone: administer prednisone tablet 50 mg PO to adults and children > 6 years of age (per MCA selection — if available); if prednisone not available, patient is < 6 y/o, or patient unable to receive PO: administer methylprednisolone IV/IO/IM (Specialist skill): Adults: 125 mg; Pediatrics: 2 mg/kg (max 125 mg)
Notify Medical Control of patient's medical history
Refer to Adult or Pediatric Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol if altered mental status present

mca_selections_required.description: MCA may approve prednisone PO as an additional medication option for adults and children > 6 y/o

key_drugs.name: NS or LR IV/IO Fluid Bolus
key_drugs.dose: Adults: up to 1 liter; Pediatrics: up to 20 mL/kg
key_drugs.indication: Adrenal crisis with hypotension and/or dehydration

key_drugs.name: Hydrocortisone Sodium Succinate (Solu-Cortef) — Patient's Own
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 100 mg IV/IM; Pediatrics: 1–2 mg/kg IV/IM
key_drugs.indication: Adrenal crisis — assist with patient's own supply
key_drugs.notes: Assist with patient's own medication. Contact Medical Control before administration.

key_drugs.name: Methylprednisolone
key_drugs.dose: Adults: 125 mg IV/IO/IM; Pediatrics: 2 mg/kg IV/IO/IM (max 125 mg)
key_drugs.indication: Adrenal crisis when patient's own hydrocortisone not available and prednisone PO not appropriate
key_drugs.notes: Use when prednisone not available, patient < 6 y/o, or patient unable to receive PO medication.

key_drugs.name: Prednisone 50 mg Tablet PO
key_drugs.dose: 50 mg PO — adults and children > 6 years of age
key_drugs.indication: Adrenal crisis when patient does not have their own hydrocortisone
key_drugs.contraindications: Patient < 6 years of age; patient unable to receive PO medication
key_drugs.scope: MCA additional medication option — requires MCA selection
key_drugs.notes: Contact Medical Control before administration. If not available or not appropriate, use methylprednisolone.

deterioration_flags: Hypotension — initiate IV/IO fluid bolus (Specialist skill); contact Medical Control
Altered mental status — refer to Altered Mental Status-Treatment Protocol
Hypoglycemia may accompany adrenal crisis — check blood glucose; treat per protocol
scope_notes: Contact Medical Control before treatment is required for all adrenal crisis patients
IV/IO access and fluid bolus: Specialist skill
Hydrocortisone (patient's own): Specialist skill to assist
Prednisone PO: MCA additional medication option (requires MCA selection)
